planned_changes: Edit [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] header to use canonical, rubric-expected column names. Proposed header (example): timestamp,row_id,value,baseline_low,baseline_high,baseline_excursion_type,in_baseline,delta_from_prev,abs_delta_from_prev,rate_violation,suggested_explanation,citation,notes
Populate 'baseline_excursion_type' values per row: 'none' for in-baseline rows (t0,t1), 'above' for t2..t5 as observed, or 'below' if applicable in other cases.
Replace string temporary stand-ins in numeric fields: set delta_from_prev and abs_delta_from_prev to null for the first row (t0) rather than 'needs_review'; keep numeric values for subsequent rows.

Convert rate_violation column entries to booleans (true/false). For t2 and t4 set true; for t1,t3,t5 set false; for t0 set null if rubric expects undefined or false if rubric demands boolean - choose per rubric and document choice in engineering_note.
Rename 'delta_from_previous' to 'delta_from_prev' (or the rubric-preferred token) and document any retained synonyms in engineering_note's schema mapping section.

Add a short CSV-schema mapping section at the top or bottom of engineering_note.md that explicitly maps CSV column names to the engineering_note terminology and justifies representation choices (null vs boolean for t0 fields).
Ensure engineering_note.md is included in full (not truncated) in the revised attempt; if the file is long, include an explicit 'full content' attachment so graders can verify edge-case wording.
Regenerate artifacts, run deterministic precheck, and re-run model grading/verifier. Attach the new grader/verifier outputs and the updated artifacts to 105-revised.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt produced correct numeric analysis and a thorough engineering note, but the CSV artifact deviates from the rubric/contract schema in ways that triggered partial credit (final_score 0.8). Primary issues: non-canonical column names, missing canonical 'baseline_excursion_type' column, first-row delta recorded as the string 'needs_review' rather than null, and non-boolean rate_violation entries. These are formatting/contract issues rather than arithmetic errors; the engineering note documents assumptions and calculations clearly, which mitigates severity.
